ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Ama ní Looti bí ki tàantí kaa làá nyā malaa. Ní bininjab bilee cúú u nì u-nimpuu nì u-bisalib bilee ŋalii ki dáaĺ-bi ki nyántì kitiŋ kpaanceetiŋu. Di sá yii ti-Dindaan nìn là kí fīī-u ní.
translated: Ama and Looti were no longer there. The bininjab people, the ones who were their leaders and their followers, the ŋalii who had previously been their guides, all had gone away. Then they arrived at Dindaan but could not find them.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Míǹ boonee ní bi pɔ́ĺ ki búntì Eeni Miisipati, kitiŋ kin bi yíì kɔŋkɔnnee Kadeesee. Niin ní bi jáń ki pɔ́ɔkiǹ Amaleeki yab bi-timmu kɔkɔ poon, ki kpáaǹ Amɔɔr yab bin kóò Asaasɔn-Taamaar tiŋkinee.
translated: And they went to the land of Eeni Miisipati, and they encamped near Kadeesee. Then they fought against the Amaleeki and completely defeated them, and captured the city of Asaasɔn-Taamaar.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Ní ki dū ijɔfi ki tɔ́ bin kɔkɔ nìn dɔ́miń ki yú kudii nyɔkɔpuee, ki yōoǹ binaacimpɔmbi ki tin cáa sāā bitikpilib, ní baa ti wāĺ ní ki nyàab̄ kipunyɔkɔ ki gīī.
translated: If you wish to speak to the one who went home to eat the special meal, you must carry the documents to the office, and upon reaching the water, wash your hands and proceed.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Ní ki bí-u yii: «Kpèè, m-tiŋki di dɔ̄ a-nimbiin nee, lēē laakin ní a lèe kí nīn kóò.»
translated: Hey, I want to go with you to help you there, so that you can do what you need to do.

ref: Then she took Esau's best clothes and put them on Jacob.
text: Reebeeka ŋá tijin ki dóòyee ní ki dū u-jipɔŋkpiliu Eesayuu bɔkutimɔntiŋ ŋin bí u-cee kudiinee ki dūu pēeǹ u-jipɔɔn uwaal Jaakɔb.
translated: Rebekah saw him blessing Esau, and then Jacob came in wearing his clothes.

ref: We were out in the field, tying up bundles of wheat. Suddenly my bundle stood up, and your bundles gathered around and bowed down to it.”
text: M dámintì yii ti bí kusaau ki bōob̀ amɔpin, ní man yal yáatìi yúl̀, ní nimbi mɔpiŋŋi māntìi tú man mɔpindi ní ki lítì di-taapu.»
translated: They said that he had taken it to the village house, and that the people were looking for him, and that they would find him and bring him back to the town.

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: Maa láá dākafì míǹ m-pɔbilin ki dóò, ní Reebeeka páaǹní ki túń u-baŋki u-gbigbil pu ki dɔ́miń ki sīntì dinyimbɔbilin ki lúń nnyim, ní m mēē-u yii: <M gbāam̀-si, pɔ̄-mi nnyim ḿ nyɔ̀.>
translated: Now Abraham had a servant in the city, and Rebecca went out to draw water, her jar on her hip, to the well to fill the jar and bring it back home, and he said to her: "Let me drink a little water from your jar."

ref: and this is the story about him. Noah was the only person who lived right and obeyed God.
text: Noowa nì u-biyaamu bɔti sɔ̀. Noowa nìn sá unimɔnti ní ki bí deedee u-yuntiŋu nib kansikin. U nìn nóó Unimbɔti mmɔntiim.
translated: My father's farm is fertile. My father has a house and also has a child named kansikin. He has Unimbɔti's land.

ref: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
text: Kitiŋ gbanti sindaariyau sá umɔnti ní, ní talaalu un bi yíì bideeliyoomee nì ataŋkpamɔnti ŋin bi yíì soowamee bí niin.
translated: Only the one who desires my life did not eat the bread or drink the water of the people.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Niinee ní Jaakɔb bí Laaban yii: «M túń ditundi ki sāā m-yuntiŋu, an puee dū m-nimpuu ki tīī-mi ḿ cáá.»
translated: I have done according to your word, and now you have seen that my livestock is with me.

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Ní u-baa bālifì-u yii: «An sá sii ŋma ní?» Ní u kíí yii: «An sá man a-jipɔŋkpiliu Eesayuu.»
translated: What have you done? I have done as you told me to do to Esau.

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Ní unimpu kíí-u yii: «Ti ŋūǹ kí ŋmɔ́ isufi in yú bukpaa poonee biliŋ.
translated: A bird does not sing with another bird's voice.

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: An pu ní u cūtì Abraam jipɔɔn Isimaayeel cee ki cáá úǹ bisal Maalaati, Neebayɔti niisal ki kútì u-nimpoobi bin u pée cááyee pu. Jaakɔb damintiiku
translated: And God said to Abraham, "I have made you a father of many nations. Your name will no longer be called Abram, but Abraham, for I have made you a father of many nations. As for Ishmael, I have heard you; I will bless him, make him fruitful, and greatly multiply him. He shall be the father of twelve princes, and I will make him a great nation. But my covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sarah will bear to you at this appointed time next year." Jacob's descendants.

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: ní ki bālifì utɔntɔnlee yii: «Ŋma di sá uninja un nyánní tisatin ki túŋkiń-ti nan ní?» Ní utɔntɔnlee kíí-u yii: «M-kpiliu dee.» Niinee ní u dū ŋŋaabiiŋkaa ki bīiǹ.
translated: The chief asked the messenger: "Why is there a delay in delivering the message?" The messenger replied: "I am tired." Then the chief sent him away.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Jaakɔb bàn̄ee ní ki yíiń u-baa yii: «M-baa!» ní u-baa kíí-u yii: «M sèé. An sá sii m-jipɔɔn ŋma?»
translated: Jacob said to him, "Where are you going?" He replied, "I am going to the field. Shall I bring you something from there?"

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: M cáá inaa nì mmamu nì ipii nì iŋoobu nì bitɔntɔnliibi bininjab nì binimpoobi. An pu ní m túnní bí dāá tùkù sii m-kpiliu yii á dàkà-mi a-ŋanti kí fōō-mi.>»
translated: I am going to eat and drink and wear clothes and use the tools and instruments. I have to do this work until my work is completed.

ref: Then seven other heads of grain came up. They were thin and scorched by a wind from the desert.
text: Ní ndifiŋkpiimu mululi mun ugbuŋkoon fáá mu fáaḿee mun nyáń ki kútì idifin ipeekaayee pu.
translated: I have stopped eating sweet things because I want to abandon the harmful work already.

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Abimeeleeki fīntì kutaafaau pɔ̀lpɔlee ní ki yíiń u-tɔntɔnliibi kɔkɔ ní ki dū an bɔtee ki máafì-bi, ní tifaandi cúú-bi tikpil.
translated: After Abimelech died, they performed the funeral rites and took him to his father's tomb and buried him, and the next day they did this.

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: Á nīn bí niin kí dāá sāā ŋyunti ŋun a-maan ŋuul làá dāá sɔ̀ŋkèe.
translated: We eat each other's food in the village with the people and then we go to eat sɔ̀ŋkèe.

ref: Dinah, the daughter of Jacob and Leah, went to visit some of the women who lived nearby.
text: Dalbee, Jaakɔb bisal Diina un Leeya màl̄ee cūtì kí màntì kitiŋ kin poon bi nìn kóòyee sapɔmbi pu.
translated: Indeed, Jacob loved Dinah and Leah, but when he realized he had many children from them, he sent them away.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Ní Unimbɔti bí-u yii: «M gbìl̄, ama an kaa sá Isimaayeel bɔti ní m bíi sòoǹ, an sá a-nimpuu Saara di làá màal̀-si kibininjabiki. Á dāá pɔ̄-kì diyindi yii Isaaki. Úǹ nì u-naantiibi bin làá páanní boonee di làá fōō dipoondi din m pōoǹ dí nīn bí kí nīn cá jaanjaanee.
translated: In the book of Genesis, it says: "Abraham was distressed because they sent Ishmael away when he was still a child, and his mother Sarah was also very upset. But God said to him, 'Do not be distressed for the boy Isaac. He will become a great nation as well, for he is your descendant.'"

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Ní ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti dū tigban ki lōoǹ Adam nì u-nimpuu.
translated: The Lord God created the earth and the heavens, and Adam and his descendants.

ref: When the chief cook saw that Joseph had given a good meaning to the dream, he told Joseph, “I also had a dream. In it I was carrying three breadbaskets stacked on top of my head.
text: Bin pūul̀ ubɔti kpɔnɔbee kpiliu nín ká yii Jooseefi tùkū u-bɔɔ u-damintiiku taapu an ŋān̄ee, ní u mun bí Jooseefi yii: «Ma mun dámintì yii bikpɔnɔmɔntiibi tikabiti tita ŋɔ́kɔ́ tɔb pu ní m tú.
translated: When he came to his brothers at Shechem, they conspired against Joseph and said, "Here comes the dreamer! Let us throw him into a pit and say a wild animal devoured him. Then we will see what becomes of his dreams."

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Míǹ ní Unimbɔti ŋá iwalfikpaan ili: ŋkpaŋkpaan di sá ŋwìiń, kutaa yaa wúntèe, ŋú nīn bí ki ŋmíntí. Kiwaai di sá uŋmal, kunyeeuee, ú nīn bí ki ŋmíntí, ní ki ŋá iŋmalbijaa mun.
translated: The word of Unimbɔti created the world: the heavens he established firm, the earth he made to flourish, and he did not abandon them. His commandments he placed, holy and unchanging, and he will not violate his covenant forever.

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: kisubiki báà kiba kaa nìn dáá nyáń kitiŋ pu, ní kumoou báà kubɔ mun kaa nìn dáá nyáń, kun puee ti-Dindaan Unimbɔti kaa nìn dáá yāntì utaa nìn̄. Unil mun nìn kpá kí kpɔ̀ tikpaal kitiŋ pu.
translated: Kisubiki did his work well, and Kumoou did his work well too, but Dindaan Unimbɔti did his work poorly. Unil did not complete his task well.
